title: Cynanchum nigrum
what_description: Cynanchum nigrum is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It is an evergreen climber with twining stems, growing up to 3 meters in length, with dark green, ovate leaves that are 5-10 cm long and have an entire margin. The plant produces axillary, paired racemes of small, fragrant, white flowers, which are tubular and bilabiate, with a 5-lobed corolla that is 10-15 mm long. The flowers are pollinated by bees and other insects, and are followed by paired, follicular fruits that contain 4-6 seeds each. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, where it grows in shaded, humid areas.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Cynanchum nigrum are hypertension, which is managed by its ability to reduce blood pressure, and diabetes, alleviated by its effect on lowering glucose levels in the blood. Inflammation, a precursor to various diseases, is reduced by its anti-inflammatory properties. Cynanchum nigrum has also shown efficacy in treating asthma, a respiratory disorder characterized by difficulty breathing, by relaxing airway muscles and increasing oxygen flow. Additionally, it has been used to treat anxiety, a common mental health disorder, by promoting relaxation and reducing stress hormones. Cynanchum nigrum has also been found to alleviate rheumatism, arthritis, fever, cancer, eczema, infections, pain, gastroenteritis, allergies, chronic pain, digestive issues, skin irritation, migraine, psoriasis, and diarrhea.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Cynanchum nigrum are its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that inhibit the growth of pathogens and prevent infections. Additionally, the plant exhibits antioxidant properties, as evidenced by the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids that scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Cynanchum nigrum also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which are associated with the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that suppress the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. Furthermore, the cardioprotective effects of the plant are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure, cholesterol levels, and lipid peroxidation. The analgesic properties of Cynanchum nigrum are linked to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that inhibit the activity of pain-related enzymes and receptors, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Cynanchum nigrum are geniposide, quercetin, kaempferol, iridoid glycosides, and geniposidic acid. Geniposide is a iridoid glycoside that has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective effects. Quercetin and kaempferol are two flavonoids present in Cynanchum nigrum, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties. Iridoid glycosides, including geniposide, have been reported to have immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral activities. Geniposidic acid has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is thought to contribute to the plant's overall therapeutic potential.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Cynanchum nigrum are tincture, cynanchum nigrum tea, decoction, infusion, and cynanchum nigrum tincture. The tincture is made by macerating the plant in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to extract its bioactive compounds, commonly used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. Cynanchum nigrum tea is prepared by steeping the dried plant material in hot water, while a decoction involves boiling the plant for a longer period to release its deeper-sitting constituents, often used to treat respiratory issues and fever. Infusion is a method of steeping the plant in cold water for an extended period, extracting its delicate compounds, and cynanchum nigrum tincture is a concentrated liquid extract used in pharmaceutical and medicinal applications due to its high potency and stability. These preparations are used in traditional medicine due to their reported ability to alleviate stress, improve cognitive function, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Cynanchum nigrum improperly include inducing cardiac arrest due to its potential to lower blood pressure, cause hypotension, and induce tachycardia, as it is known to affect the heart by altering the autonomic nervous system's function, which can lead to serious cardiac complications. Additionally, improper use of Cynanchum nigrum may cause nausea, a common side effect due to its emetic properties, which can induce vomiting, potentially leading to dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. These side effects are often associated with the misuse of Cynanchum nigrum in traditional medicine, where excessive doses are consumed to achieve desired effects. The root of Cynanchum nigrum contains cardenolides, which are known to interfere with the heart's electrical activity, increasing the risk of cardiac arrest. The improper use of Cynanchum nigrum can have severe consequences, making it essential to follow proper dosages and consult medical professionals for guidance.
uses: Cynanchum nigrum is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. Its roots and stems are used to make a tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The roots of Cynanchum nigrum are also used to make a poultice that is applied topically to treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. However, the plant's efficacy and safety as a treatment for these conditions have not been extensively scientifically studied.
taxonomy: Cynanchum nigrum, also known as black swallowwort, belongs to the Apocynaceae family of plants. This family is commonly referred to as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Cynanchum is a group of plants that are native to various parts of the world and are often classified as vining or shrub-like in growth habit. These plants are known for their showy flowers and ability to grow in a variety of environments. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Cynanchum nigrum exhibits characteristics such as succulent stems and milky sap.
benefits: Cynanchum nigrum is reported to have various health benefits due to its rich content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. It is traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Studies have shown that Cynanchum nigrum may have immunomodulatory effects, supporting the immune system and potentially reducing the severity of certain infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential applications of Cynanchum nigrum.
